title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Easy Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe (Authentic Chinese)

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked homemade Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao with a golden, crispy topping on a cooling rack.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, fluffy Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) straight from your oven – a classic Chinese bakery treat!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic, known for their sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's skin, despite containing no actual pineapple. These soft, fluffy buns are a perfect treat for breakfast or a snack, offering a delightful contrast between the rich, sugary crust and the tender bread beneath. Enjoy the comforting aroma and taste of these iconic Chinese pastries baked fresh in your own kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine 300g all-purpose flour, 40g granulated sugar, 5g instant dry yeast, 10g milk powder, and 3g salt. Add 1 large room temperature egg and 120ml warm milk. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a lightly floured surface for about 5 minutes.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Add 30g softened unsalted butter to the dough. Continue kneading for another 10-15 minutes until the dough is smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the pineapple topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 60g softened unsalted butter and 80g granulated sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg yolk and 1 tablespoon of milk. Gradually add 120g all-purpose flour and mix until a soft dough forms. Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, each weighing approximately 60-70g. Roll each piece into a smooth, round ball. Arrange the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving enough space between them.

instructions.title: Apply Topping and Second Proofing
instructions.text: Remove the chilled pineapple topping dough from the refrigerator and divide it into 8 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, flatten each portion into a thin disk, about 3-4 inches in diameter. Carefully place a topping disk over each dough bun, covering about two-thirds of the bun. Use a knife to lightly score a crosshatch pattern on the topping. Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Lightly brush the exposed dough (not the topping) with the beaten egg wash. Bake the Pineapple Buns for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. Remove from the oven and transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly.

tips: For extra fluffy buns, ensure your yeast is active and your milk is warm (not hot) to activate it properly.
To achieve a perfect crispy topping, make sure the butter in the topping mixture is softened but not melted. Chilling the topping dough also helps with handling.
Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven. If storing, reheat them gently in a toaster oven for a few minutes to restore crispiness.
article:
Mastering the art of Chinese baking brings a unique satisfaction, and few creations rival the iconic Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao. This beloved pastry, with its signature golden, crumbly topping and soft, fluffy interior, is a staple in bakeries and dim sum restaurants across Hong Kong and beyond. Despite its name, the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao contains no actual pineapple; its moniker comes from the distinctive cracked, checkered appearance of its crust, which strikingly resembles the skin of a pineapple.

Imagine biting into a warm, freshly baked bun: the sweet, slightly crunchy crust shatters gently, giving way to a light, airy bread that melts in your mouth. Whether enjoyed plain, with a slab of cold butter, or as part of a hearty breakfast, the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao offers a comforting taste of authentic Chinese bakery traditions. While it might seem intimidating to make at home, this guide will walk you through every step, ensuring you can recreate this delicious classic in your own kitchen. Get ready to impress your family and friends with your homemade Bo Lo Bao!

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

The Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, known as "Bo Lo Bao" in Cantonese, is a sweet bun that originated in Hong Kong during the 1940s. Its widespread popularity has made it an emblem of Hong Kongese culinary culture. The "pineapple" aspect refers purely to the aesthetic of its topping. This topping is a crisp, sugary crust, usually made from a mixture of sugar, eggs, flour, and butter, which is piped or spread over the unbaked bun dough. As the bun bakes, this topping expands and cracks, creating the characteristic pineapple-like pattern.

Beneath this delightful crust lies a rich, tender, and slightly sweet yeast bread. The contrast between the crunchy, sweet exterior and the soft, fluffy interior is what makes the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao so utterly addictive. Traditionally, it's served warm, often sliced open and filled with a thick slice of cold butter (known as "Bo Lo Yau"), creating a wonderful interplay of temperatures and textures. It’s a popular breakfast item, afternoon tea snack, or a delightful treat at any time of day.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao requires two main sets of ingredients: one for the soft, airy bun dough and another for the iconic, crispy topping.

For the Dough:
*   High-protein bread flour: 300g (or all-purpose flour for a softer crumb)
*   Instant dry yeast: 4g (about 1 teaspoon)
*   Granulated sugar: 40g
*   Salt: 3g (about ½ teaspoon)
*   Whole milk: 120ml (warmed to around 100-110°F / 38-43°C)
*   Large egg: 1 (at room temperature)
*   Unsalted butter: 30g (softened)

For the Topping:
*   All-purpose flour: 100g
*   Granulated sugar: 60g
*   Unsalted butter: 60g (softened)
*   Large egg yolk: 1 (at room temperature)
*   Whole milk or evaporated milk: 1 tablespoon
*   Custard powder (optional): 1 teaspoon (for enhanced flavor and color)
*   Egg wash (for brushing buns before topping): 1 egg yolk + 1 tbsp milk

Ensure all your ingredients are fresh and at the specified temperatures for optimal results.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a rewarding process that involves careful attention to detail at each stage.

1.  Prepare the Dough:
    *   In a large bowl, whisk together the bread flour, sugar, salt, and instant yeast.
    *   In a separate bowl, whisk the warmed milk and egg.
    *   Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a spatula until a shaggy dough forms.
    *   Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook for 7-8 minutes.
    *   Gradually incorporate the softened butter, kneading until fully absorbed and the dough is very smooth and passes the "windowpane test" (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
    *   Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

2.  Shape the Buns:
    *   Gently punch down the proofed dough to release air.
    *   Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces (about 60-70g each).
    *   Roll each piece into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each bun.
    *   Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof for a second time in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

3.  Make the Topping:
    *   While the buns are on their second proof, cream the softened butter and sugar in a bowl until light and fluffy.
    *   Beat in the egg yolk and milk (and custard powder if using) until well combined.
    *   Gradually add the all-purpose flour, mixing until just combined and a soft dough forms. Do not overmix.
    *   Gather the topping dough, flatten it slightly, wrap it in plastic wrap, and chill in the refrigerator for at least 15-20 minutes to firm up.

4.  Assemble and Bake:
    *   Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
    *   Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of pieces as your buns.
    *   On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, roll out each piece of topping dough into a thin circle, slightly larger than the top of your buns.
    *   Carefully peel a topping circle and place it over the top of each proofed bun, gently pressing down to adhere to the sides.
    *   Using a knife or a dough scraper, gently score a cross-hatch pattern on the top of the pineapple crust, mimicking the look of a pineapple. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bun itself.
    *   Brush any exposed parts of the bun (not the topping) with the egg wash for a golden shine.
    *   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the buns are cooked through.
    *   Transfer the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving bakery-quality Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Kneading is Key:** Don't skimp on kneading the dough. A well-kneaded dough will be smooth, elastic, and pass the windowpane test, resulting in a wonderfully soft and airy bun. A stand mixer greatly simplifies this process.
*   **Patience with Proofing:** Allow the dough to proof fully in both stages. Rushing this process will result in dense buns. A warm, humid environment (like an oven with the light on) can help.
*   **Chill the Topping:** The topping dough needs to be firm enough to handle and roll out without being too sticky. Chilling it briefly makes it much easier to work with and ensures a crispier crust.
*   **Don't Overmix the Topping:** Overmixing the topping can develop gluten, making it tough rather than crumbly. Mix until just combined.
*   **Even Scoring:** When scoring the pineapple pattern, aim for even, gentle cuts. This helps the topping crack beautifully and consistently during baking.
*   **Oven Temperature:** Ensure your oven is preheated to the correct temperature. An accurate oven thermometer can be very helpful. Bake until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through, but avoid overbaking, which can dry them out.

## Serving Suggestions

The classic way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is undoubtedly warm, fresh from the oven, and sliced horizontally with a generous, thick slab of cold butter inserted in the middle. The melting butter against the warm bun creates a sublime experience known as "Bo Lo Yau" (Pineapple Butter Bun).

Beyond this traditional serving, Bo Lo Bao is also fantastic:
*   As a simple breakfast pastry with a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea or coffee.
*   A delightful afternoon snack with a light beverage.
*   As part of a larger dim sum spread.
*   Plain, allowing the natural sweetness and textures to shine.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is often enjoyed plain or with butter, there are several popular variations you can explore:

*   **Fillings:** You can easily incorporate fillings into your Bo Lo Bao. Popular choices include sweet custard cream, red bean paste, or even savory BBQ pork (char siu). To add a filling, flatten each dough ball after the first proof, place a spoonful of filling in the center, and then pinch the dough to seal it before shaping it back into a ball for the second proof.
*   **Flour:** For an even chewier texture in the bun, you can use a higher protein bread flour. If you prefer a lighter, cake-like crumb, a blend of all-purpose and cake flour can be experimented with.
*   **Milk Powder:** Adding a tablespoon of milk powder to your bun dough can enhance its richness and flavor.
*   **Custard Powder in Topping:** A little custard powder in the topping (as suggested in the ingredients) gives it a more vibrant yellow color and a subtle vanilla-like flavor, which is common in many Hong Kong bakeries.

Experimenting with these variations can add new dimensions to your homemade Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, though the classic, unfilled version remains a timeless favorite.

Creating your own Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao at home is a truly rewarding experience. From the careful kneading of the dough to the satisfying crack of the golden crust, each step brings you closer to a beloved piece of Chinese baking heritage. This iconic pastry, with its irresistible combination of soft bread and sweet, crunchy topping, is more than just a bun; it's a comforting slice of culinary tradition. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to enjoy the authentic taste of a homemade Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao!
